instruction: Open a terminal (xfce4-terminal). In the terminal, run: curl http://localhost:8000/data.csv -o /home/tester/data.csv. Then open the file /home/tester/data.csv in gedit. Use Find and Replace (Ctrl+H) to replace all occurrences of 'ERROR' with 'FIXED'. Save the file. Then switch back to the terminal and run: grep -c FIXED /home/tester/data.csv
completion_condition: The file data.csv has been downloaded, all ERROR entries replaced with FIXED, saved, and the grep count command has been executed in the terminal.

config.command:
mkdir -p /home/tester/server && cat > /home/tester/server/data.csv << 'CSVEOF'
id,timestamp,status,message
1,2024-01-15 08:30:00,OK,System started
2,2024-01-15 08:31:12,ERROR,Connection timeout on port 5432
3,2024-01-15 08:32:45,OK,Heartbeat received
4,2024-01-15 08:33:01,ERROR,Disk usage above threshold
5,2024-01-15 08:34:22,OK,Backup completed
6,2024-01-15 08:35:10,ERROR,Authentication failed for user admin
7,2024-01-15 08:36:00,OK,Cache cleared
8,2024-01-15 08:37:15,ERROR,Memory allocation failure in worker 3
9,2024-01-15 08:38:44,OK,Service restored
10,2024-01-15 08:39:59,ERROR,SSL certificate expiring in 7 days
CSVEOF

metadata.description: Multi-app workflow: open terminal, curl a CSV from a local server, open in gedit, find-and-replace ERROR->FIXED, save, then verify in terminal. Tests app switching, terminal interaction, and dialog navigation.
